commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bd2dfdbaebffb58171bd9ff71f3361bbc3a939d9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds trailing `notify=false` parsing inside `sendMessageTelegram`, applies marker-derived silent options to text/rich/media sends, and adds focused Telegram send tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +65. Total +71 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main prompts/records `notify=false` heartbeat intent but Telegram sends only honor structured `silent` metadata, and the reported marker parser is absent from current Telegram delivery. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external real-behavior proof gate is unmet and the remaining delivery semantics need contributor or maintainer repair before merge.

target.title: fix(telegram): strip trailing notify=false marker and send as silent
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: LLMs append `\nnotify=false` to heartbeat/subagent completion text to indicate silent delivery. Previously this marker leaked into delivered messages with notifications enabled (issue #80569).
- Solution: Strip the trailing notify=false marker at the entry of `sendMessageTelegram` — the single convergence point for all Telegram text sends — before chunking. Derive `effectiveSilent` from both `opts.silent` and marker presence.
- What changed: `extensions/telegram/src/send.ts` (add regex + marker stripping + effectiveSilent derivation, replace 3 disable_notification spread points and 3 logging lines), `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts` (3 new regression tests).
- What did NOT change: `deliverReplies` delivery logic, `sendPollTelegram`, outbound adapter, heartbeat runner, or any other delivery paths.

Fixes #80569

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram text ending with `\nnotify=false` should be sent with `disable_notification: true` and the marker stripped from the text.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v24.15.0, worktree SHA bd2dfdb
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node --import tsx -e '
  const { sendMessageTelegram } = await import("./extensions/telegram/src/send.js");
  function mk() { const c = []; return { fn: (...a) => (c.push(a), Promise.resolve({ message_id: 1, chat: { id: a[0] } })), c }; }
  const cfg = { openclaw: { configDir: "/tmp" }, gateway: {} };
  const m1 = mk();
  await sendMessageTelegram("123", "done\nnotify=false", { cfg, token: "t", api: { sendMessage: m1.fn } });
  console.log("notify=false:", m1.c[0][1]==="done" && m1.c[0][2]?.disable_notification===true ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
  const m2 = mk();
  await sendMessageTelegram("123", "normal", { cfg, token: "t", api: { sendMessage: m2.fn } });
  console.log("no marker:", m2.c[0][1]==="normal" && m2.c[0][2]?.disable_notification===undefined ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
  const m3 = mk();
  await sendMessageTelegram("123", "ok\nNotify=FALSE", { cfg, token: "t", api: { sendMessage: m3.fn } });
  console.log("case-insens:", m3.c[0][1]==="ok" && m3.c[0][2]?.disable_notification===true ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
  '
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=123 messageId=1 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text silent=true chunkCount=1
  notify=false: PASS
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=123 messageId=1 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1
  no marker: PASS
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=123 messageId=1 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text silent=true chunkCount=1
  case-insens: PASS
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The notify=false marker is stripped from the text before `api.sendMessage()` is called. `disable_notification: true` is set in the API params when the marker is present (including case-insensitive variants). When no marker is present, `disable_notification` is not set. The runtime logs correctly show `silent=true` when the marker triggers silent delivery.
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw gateway instance or use a real Telegram bot token. The fix is a pure text-preprocessing change (regex match + strip + boolean flag) at the entry of `sendMessageTelegram`, before any API calls. Verified by calling the real patched function via `node --import tsx` with a mock `api.sendMessage` to inspect the exact params passed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Trailing notify=false marker stripped and disable_notification set; case-insensitive matching; normal text without marker unchanged
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The fix is a text-preprocessing check at the single convergence point for all Telegram sends; asserting the stripped text and API params covers all delivery paths.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The LLM prompt instructs models to append `\nnotify=false` to heartbeat/subagent completion text, but the Telegram send path had no handler to strip this marker or convert it to `disable_notification: true`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No text preprocessing at the send entry point to detect and strip the trailing notify=false convention before chunking and API dispatch.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
